tasks.Task: Create budget covering appliances, furniture, utilities, car, yard, security, contingency
Collect passports, birth certificates, marriage certificate, school/kindergarten confirmations
Collect medical and vaccination records for all children
Create digital folder with all family/property documents
Build master contact list (developer, lawyer, utilities, school/KG, installers, insurance, car providers)
Ask developer for updated construction timeline
Ask developer to confirm what will be complete by handover
Request kitchen drawings and appliance niche dimensions
Request PV installer details, inverter model, monitoring app details
Request pool cover instructions and handover process
Request water softener / under-sink filtration installer contact
Confirm utility meter/account readiness for activation or transfer
Confirm responsibility matrix: snag fixes, PV commissioning, pool handover, AC commissioning, water systems
Shortlist landscaping companies for Phase 1 yard setup
Compare used-car options and rental fallback options
Prepare appliance list: fridge/freezer, oven, hob, extractor, dishwasher, washer, dryer, microwave, kettle, toaster
Prepare furniture list: beds/cots, mattresses, sofa, dining set, storage, basic outdoor seating, blackout solutions
Prepare child-safety list: stair gates, cabinet locks, socket covers, anchors, baby monitors, blackout items, high chairs
Confirm bedding/nap requirements for little ones
Confirm emergency contact details on file
Confirm whether there is a gradual adaptation process for younger children
Prepare first-week routine plan for mornings and pickups
Weekly follow-up with developer on build progress
Weekly follow-up on supplier delivery dates
Assign owners to all open tasks
By mid-July: ensure all essential orders are placed
By mid-July: ensure all critical service appointments are scheduled
Confirm house progress against expected handover
Test school/kindergarten commute from the house
Walk the entire house and exterior
Compare actual build to contract appendix
Confirm AC units installed/planned in correct locations
Confirm wardrobes/storage are in place as expected
Confirm kitchen cabinetry layout matches drawings
Check pool and pool cover progress
Check roof access and roof room/bathroom readiness
Assess yard condition and scope for Phase 1 landscaping
Talk to internet provider or local rep
Talk to water softener / filtration installer
Talk to used-car dealer or rental provider
Decide whether to install cameras before move
Decide whether to do temporary landscaping only or more
Decide whether to buy or rent a car initially
Decide whether blackout solutions will be temporary or permanent before move-in
Drive route from house to school/kindergarten at morning time
Note actual travel time in morning traffic
Identify easiest family route for drop-off / return
Confirm final included items against contract appendix
Test all windows and mosquito nets
Classify each issue: critical / important / cosmetic
Assign responsible party for each issue
Record promised completion date for each issue
Confirm whether any PV metering/utility step is still pending
Confirm whether physical presence is needed for water-system installer
Order basic outdoor seating if feasible
Order router / mesh Wi-Fi if needed
Book initial yard works if proceeding
Capture delivery dates for all essentials
Identify furniture that must be anchored
Decide on camera coverage for outdoor/pool area
Decide where monitors/night lights will go
Inspect used-car options or confirm rental route
Confirm timing for delivery or pickup
Decide whether a bridge rental is needed for first weeks
Confirm cosmetic snags are acceptable for move-in
Confirm all appliances installed and working
Confirm sofa and dining setup in place
Confirm AC works in all rooms
Put mattresses, bedding, protectors in place
Set up one calm play area
Buy toilet paper, soap/shampoo, cleaning supplies
Buy laundry detergent, dish soap, garbage bags
Buy basic cookware, plates, cups, cutlery
Stock snacks, milk, breakfast basics, freezer basics
Stock bottled water initially if needed
Remove packaging / trip hazards / sharp objects
Make yard safe for supervised play
Ensure yard will not become muddy/impossible after irrigation or cleaning
Pick up car or confirm rental
Drive from house at morning time
Decide backup plan if a child is tired/sick/upset
Reconfirm all deliveries and installation dates
Arrange grocery delivery or first shopping plan
Prepare carry-on folder with all critical documents
Confirm airport transfer and child-seat logistics if needed
Confirm basic supplies are in the house
Pack one set of sheets/pajamas easily accessible
Check AC in all sleeping rooms
Test school/kindergarten routine once more if needed
Confirm all documents/bags ready for first day
Identify nearest pharmacy / pediatric support / supermarket
Lay out clothes the night before
Expect tiredness and some emotional overload
Avoid stacking social commitments during first week

trip_schedule.Primary purpose: Pre-handover planning, measurements, supplier selection, commute test
Final setup, safety, stock house, car, school routine dry run
Settle in, keep routines calm, launch school/kindergarten smoothly
